sections.paragraph:
ἀνακοινόω 1 aor. mid. ptc. ἀνακοινωσαμένου 2 Macc 14:20 (X. et al.; pap, usu. in mid., as Diod S 4, 40, 2; PCairZen 520, 6 [III b.c.]; 2 Macc 14:20; Jos., Ant. 19, 19) to make another privy to what one knows, communicate τινί (τι) (something) to someone Dg 8:9 (ἀνακοινοῦν τινί τι Syntipas p. 9, 17; 47, 8).—DELG s.v. κοινός.

aor. aor. = aorist

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

usu. usu. = usually

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

PCairZen PCairZen = Zenon Papyri, Catalogue général . . . du Musée du Caire—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Syntipas Syntipas , a collection of fables under the name of Syntapis; before XI a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 65
